page: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets. This instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It also includes 307 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test at 1898.99 and Geekbench single-core score of 1648.0, rank in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3539.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3869.36. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached read and write speeds of 30.58 GB/sec and 25.35 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers.

The E8-4ds_v5 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the constrained CPU core count and weak multi-threaded performance, as evidenced by a poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 19.79 GB/sec. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications with low parallel compute demands, such as caching layers, relational database hosting with moderate query volumes, and lightweight text processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance within the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks like Geekbench and stress-ng show average performance, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower tiers. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand intensive parallel processing. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is an Intel-based memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, offering average single-core but weak multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage on Intel Xeon hardware.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high RAM-to-vCPU allocation and local storage for memory-bound workloads with moderate parallel processing needs.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth limits parallel throughput
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
